title: European markets set to slump at the open as Iran war intensifies
text: European stocks are expected to slump at the open on Thursday as the Iran war escalates following attacks on Iranian and Qatari energy infrastructure.

title: What Today's Market Decline Portends
text: I think today's market decline portends even lower prices going forward. It's not the magnitude of the decline that I'm concerned about, but the fact that we closed more than 5% below the all-time high for the S&P 500.

title: Powell says not as much progress being made on inflation as 'hoped'
text: The major averages slid to their lows of the day as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said that the central bank wasn't making as much progress on bringing down inflation as it had hoped. “The forecast is that we will be making progress on inflation, not as much as we had hoped, but some progress on inflation,” Powell said at his press conference.

title: Powell says U.S. economy is not experiencing stagflation
text: With fears of higher oil prices heating up inflation while weakening growth, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said that he wouldn't use the term “stagflation” to describe the U.S. economy.

title: What the Federal Reserve's rate decision means for investors
text: Today, the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, keeping 1 rate cut in play this year as uncertainty mounts. The central bank voted in a split decision on Wednesday to hold its benchmark interest rate in the range of 3.5% to 3.75%.

title: Fed's Powell says it's 'too soon to know' Iran war's impact on economy

title: Dow falls 700 pts as Fed holds rates, hot PPI fuels inflation fears
text: Wall Street ended sharply lower on Wednesday as investors reacted to the Federal Reserve's decision to hold interest rates steady, rising inflation pressures, and escalating geopolitical risks linked to the Middle East conflict. Major US indexes extended losses following the Fed's announcement and fresh economic data.

text: The Fed opted to hold rates steady at its March meeting, as expected. Chair Jerome Powell talked about jobs, inflation, oil prices, and AI productivity gains.

title: U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Rise by 6.2 Million Barrels
text: Commercial crude-oil stockpiles rose by 6.2 million barrels last week. Analysts had predicted crude stockpiles would decline by roughly 40,000 barrels.

title: It's Starting To Feel A Lot Like 2007
text: Current market conditions closely resemble the pre-crisis environment of 2007 in several primary ways, raising caution for investors. Echoes of the subprime crisis and official reassurances highlight systemic risks that may not be fully appreciated.

title: March Fed Meeting: Policymakers Stay The Course Despite Negative Externalities
text: The March FOMC meeting reflected continued stability despite the multitude of negative externalities. The negative externalities include declining payrolls, rising oil prices, and a highly uncertain geopolitical world outlook.

title: Powell: Oil shock 'part of' higher inflation projection for 2026
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell explains what factors in the economy are contributing to the higher inflation projection for 2026.

title: Companies See Inflation Inching Up to 2.1%, Atlanta Fed Says
text: Firms' year-ahead inflation expectations increased by 0.2 percentage points to 2.1% in March, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta's March Business Inflation Expectations (BIE) Survey. That figure is up from the 1.9% recorded in the February BIE Survey but down from the 2.5% recorded in the March 2025 survey.

title: Fed warns Middle East tensions could shake markets as it holds rates
text: US equities held steady on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged, signaling caution amid persistent inflation and growing geopolitical risks in the Middle East. The Fed maintained its target range for the federal funds rate at 3.50%–3.75%, in line with market expectations.

title: Powell: Iran war to increase inflation in the near term
text: Surging oil prices due to the Iran war are expected to increase inflation in the near term, Federal Reserve Chair Powell said. “Near-term measures of inflation expectations have risen in recent weeks, likely reflecting the substantial rise in oil prices caused by the supply disruptions in the Middle East,” the Fed chair said.

title: Crude Awakening
text: Oil price volatility surges as bombs fall in the Middle East Be ready for more wild swings in oil and gas prices. The Cboe Crude Oil ETF Volatility Index (OVX), which measures implied volatility in oil ETF options, estimates the expected volatility of crude oil prices over the next 30 days.

title: Powell delivers his most definitive answer yet on how long he'll stay on as Fed chair
text: Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell said on Wednesday that he will stay on as “chair pro tem” if his successor Kevin Warsh is not confirmed by mid-May when Powell's term expires.

title: Powell Says He Has No Intention to Leave Fed Until DOJ Probe Over
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says he plans to stay at the Fed until after the Justice Department's investigation is complete during a news conference after the central bank's policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee to leave interest rates unchanged.

title: Fed Chair Powell: If no new Fed chair is confirmed by end of my term I will serve as 'chair pro tem'
text: Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell answers questions following the FOMC's decision to leave the Federal Funds rate unchanged.
